etymology.description: phonetic 爻 + phonetic 六 -> 𦥑 added -> semantic 子 added -> simplified 六 -> final form
etymology.interpretation: The character 學 (xué, "study") originally combined two phonetic components, 爻 (yáo, "double cross") and 六 (liù, "six"), to represent its sound. Over time, the character evolved with the addition of 𦥑 (components positioned like hands around 爻) and the semantic 子 (zǐ, "child") to specify a child learning or studying, under a cover (冖). In its modern form, 六 is simplified to resemble 冖, which visually merges with the hands around 爻, reflecting the learning process enclosed as children gather to study.
etymology.simplification: "学" is the simplified form of "學." The transformation occurred by merging the visual elements into a compact form, where 冖 serves as a common phonetic and visual element, while the complex structure of 學 was streamlined.

examples.chinese: 他每天都努力学习(他每天都努力學習)。
我在北京大学学习中文(我在北京大學學習中文)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài Běijīng Dàxué xuéxí zhōngwén.
examples.english: I am studying Chinese at Peking University.

examples.chinese: 这个学校有很多优秀的老师(這個學校有很多優秀的老師)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xuéxiào yǒu hěnduō yōuxiù de lǎoshī.
examples.english: This school has many excellent teachers.
